questions: For bug #1 (re-rendering): Should we reduce the poll interval from 2s to something longer (e.g. 10s), or keep it at 2s for multi-tab sync fidelity? Reducing it would help re-render frequency but hurt multi-tab responsiveness.

For bug #3 (render button): Is there a planned render backend on the roadmap? If so, should we keep the render infrastructure (status tracking, progress state) intact, or strip it all out as dead code?
For bug #5 (drag from GenerationsPane): Do you know if this ever worked, or was it only partially implemented? This affects whether I'm debugging a regression vs. completing a feature.
success_criteria: Player plays continuously without stopping/restarting during idle editing and debounced saves
Navigating to /tools/video-editor auto-loads the most recent (or newly created) timeline without showing a list page
Back button in editor navigates to / (home)
Render button shows a clear 'not available yet' toast instead of setting a confusing error state
Dragging a generation from GenerationsPane to AssetPanel registers the asset; dragging to timeline creates a clip
Inline text editing textarea displays text at the same visual scale as the rendered composition
Play/pause controls are always clickable regardless of overlay positions
During playback, only PreviewPanel and OverlayEditor re-render per frame; TimelineEditor and PropertiesPanel remain stable
assumptions: The primary cause of Player re-rendering is resolvedConfig identity instability from React Query poll cycles (2s interval) — not React 18 strict mode (strict mode only double-renders in dev, not prod)
The signature-based memoization of resolvedConfig will be sufficient to stabilize Player props without needing to disable polling
The TimelineList component and timeline CRUD operations should be preserved (not deleted) even though the list page is removed — they may be useful for multi-timeline management later
The text editing 'small' issue is a font-size scaling problem (composition fontSize not mapped to screen pixels), not a coordinate mapping bug
The play button z-index fix can be done with CSS only (no structural DOM changes needed)
SupabaseDataProvider.registerAsset exists and works — the drag-drop issue is more likely in the drag data format or event handling than the persistence layer
